name: Alex Chen
current_workflow: Shoots 50+ videos a week on an iPhone 15 Pro. Uses CapCut templates blindly, then spends 2 hours manually adjusting cuts in Premiere Pro because the AI captions miss context. Watches analytics daily, heartbroken when CTR drops below 1%.
personality: High-energy, data-obsessed, views every algorithm update as a personal mission.
triggers: A case study showing a 200% increase in 3-second retention rates.
objections: Existing AI tools like Opus Clip are 'good enough' for repurposing, but I need something specific for original hooks.
current_tools: iPhone 15 Pro, CapCut, Premiere Pro, Notion, Later

name: Sarah Jenkins
current_workflow: Co-owner of a 4-person agency. Manages editing remotely. Relies on her editor who says 'it feels right,' but they don't look at retention graphs. Consistently misses the 'hook' concept, treating it like a standard intro.
personality: Pragmatic, results-driven, skeptical of 'magic buttons' but desperate for growth.
triggers: ROI proof: 'Show me how this tool saved our last video edit by 40% minutes.'
objections: We already have a retainer editor; why pay for another SaaS subscription?
current_tools: DaVinci Resolve, Google Workspace, Asana, Buffer

name: Marcus Thorne
current_workflow: Oversees a team of 3 video editors. Uses rigid corporate style guides that kill viral potential. Hates the 'binary' nature of TikTok; wants safety over virality. Often cancels campaigns because the 'hook' feels too 'influencer-y' for their brand.
personality: Corporate, cautious, values brand safety above all else, slow to adopt new trends.
triggers: Social proof from a B2B company that successfully used hooks to drive leads.
objections: TikTok is for Gen Z; my audience is 30+. I need to explain why this tool fits our 'professional' aesthetic.
current_tools: Adobe Creative Cloud, HubSpot, Zoom, Microsoft Teams

name: Jenny Wu
current_workflow: Films in a dorm room with a ring light. Uses TikTok's native editor and free Canva templates. Stuck at 500 views every time. Doesn't understand 'hooks,' thinks it's just 'being interesting.'
personality: Impulsive, broke, easily convinced by hype, fears missing out on the 'next big thing.'
triggers: A free trial or a 'student discount' that promises instant growth.
objections: My budget is $200 this month; I can't afford another subscription. Is this really necessary?

name: David O'Connor
current_workflow: Posts 2x daily. Uses Vizard.ai for quick edits but struggles with the 'hook' timing. Often starts videos with 'Hey guys, welcome back!' which he knows is bad but can't resist.
personality: Energetic, self-taught, believes in hard work over tools, but realizes work isn't enough.
triggers: Before/after analytics showing the exact second his old videos lost viewers.
objections: I'm great at what I do; maybe the tool is just over-engineering a simple idea.

name: Elena Rossi
current_workflow: Shoots on a GoPro, edits on a laptop while traveling. Uses generic AI clips (Runway) which feel too polished for her raw style. Struggles to make the 'hook' match her specific travel vibe.
personality: Artistic, detail-oriented, resistant to templates, prefers unique workflows.
triggers: A tool that respects her unique visual style rather than forcing a 'viral template.'
objections: I don't want my content to look like every other 'travel hack' video. Keep it authentic.

name: Tom Baker
current_workflow: Hired a nephew who films TikToks. The nephew uses CapCut but the retention is low. Tom doesn't understand why 'funny' isn't enough. Wants a tool that guarantees views for his local service.
personality: Traditional, values word-of-mouth, suspicious of 'tech-speak,' loyal to old methods.
triggers: A neighbor or local influencer endorsing the tool (Social Proof).
objections: I've never paid for a 'video tool' before. It feels like a scam to me.

name: Priya Patel
current_workflow: Repurposes YouTube long-form content for TikTok. Uses Opus Clip, but the AI often cuts off the 'hook' mid-sentence. Frustrated by the binary nature of TikTok vs. YouTube pacing.
personality: Analytical, fast-paced, hates wasted time, loves efficiency hacks.
triggers: A workflow that automates the 'hook' detection and places it perfectly.
objections: Opus Clip does 90% of the work; why do I need another tool?
current_tools: YouTube Studio, Opus Clip, Descript, Slack

name: James Wilson
current_workflow: Focuses on stream highlights. Uses generic editing apps. Doesn't care much about hooks because his personality carries the content. Willing to pay if it saves time.
personality: Casual, loyal to brands he trusts, impulsive buyer.
triggers: A discount code or a 'gamer aesthetic' UI.
objections: My gameplay is funny; I don't need a fancy hook tool.

name: Linda Chang
current_workflow: Uses stock footage and AI voiceovers. Hooks are generic ('Looking for a home?'). Low engagement. Feels behind the curve compared to younger agents.
triggers: Case study from a successful real estate influencer.
objections: This looks too 'young' for my established clientele. Will it hurt my brand?
current_tools: Zillow, LinkedIn, Microsoft Office, WhatsApp Business

name: Kevin Ross
current_workflow: Shoots 3x a week. Uses InShot. Struggles with the 'first 3 seconds.' Often shows the whole cooking process before showing the result, killing retention.
personality: Creative, passionate, hates failure, eager to learn.
triggers: A tutorial specifically on 'How to structure a food hook.'
objections: I'm a chef, not an editor. I don't want a tool that makes me think like a tech guy.

name: Rachel Green
current_workflow: Shares mental health tips. Very formal tone. Uses Canva for text overlays. Hooks are boring because she's afraid of saying the 'wrong' thing in the first 3 seconds.
personality: Empathetic, cautious, values ethics, slow to change.
triggers: Reassurance that the tool helps her stay ethical and professional.
objections: TikTok is too chaotic for serious topics like therapy.

name: Samir Khan
current_workflow: Collaborates with brands. Needs high-end look. Uses CapCut but finds the auto-captions too messy for luxury brands. Needs a hook that screams 'expensive' instantly.
triggers: A hook template that automatically adds luxury fonts and sound design.
objections: The tool must not make my video look 'cheap' or generic.

name: Bob Miller
current_workflow: Started making TikToks to combat loneliness. Uses phone camera and TikTok editor. Confused by the 'hook' concept, thinks it's just 'starting the video.'
personality: Kind, confused by tech, loyal to free tools, hates complexity.
triggers: Someone calling him 'Grandpa' and explaining it simply over the phone.
objections: My hands shake; I can't learn new apps. I just want to post and forget.

name: Zoe Martinez
current_workflow: Posts dance challenges. Needs fast cuts. Uses CapCut speed ramps but often misses the beat drop for the hook. Wants a tool that syncs the hook to the beat automatically.
personality: Spontaneous, trend-chaser, hates waiting, visual learner.
triggers: A feature that automatically finds the beat drop and cuts the video there.
objections: CapCut already has auto-beat sync; why pay more?

name: Henry Ford
current_workflow: Reviews cars. Long intros explaining the car model before showing the car. Uses generic AI tools that don't understand automotive jargon in hooks.
personality: Niche expert, loyal to his specific topic, hates generic solutions.
triggers: A tool trained specifically on automotive terminology and car sounds.
objections: Generic AI tools don't understand the nuance of car specs. It ruins my authority.
current_tools: Final Cut Pro, Soundtrap, YouTube Studio, Twitter

name: Lisa Wong
current_workflow: Urgent messaging. Needs to stop the scroll with 'Did you know?' facts immediately. Uses Canva and Instagram Reels tools, confused by TikTok specific hooks.
triggers: A tool that helps her visualize the 'problem' in the first 3 seconds.
objections: I don't want to spend money on a tool if I can just be more passionate.

name: Gary Thompson
current_workflow: Creates corporate training videos. Uses PowerPoint converted to video. No hooks, just straight lectures. Realizes he needs to adapt for TikTok but doesn't know how.
personality: Structured, process-oriented, resistant to chaos, values data.
triggers: Data showing how hooks improve corporate training completion rates.
objections: This is for professional development, not entertainment. It undermines my authority.

name: Maya Singh
current_workflow: Uses whatever is trending. Switched from CapCut to Vizard because 'it looked cooler.' Doesn't understand retention metrics, just likes the UI.
personality: Volatile, trend-hopping, impulsive, loves aesthetics over substance.
triggers: A new, shiny AI feature that looks unique on social media.
objections: If I don't see it on my FYP within 24 hours, I'm deleting the app.
